tweets.full_text:
We have never jumped on the hype train at @takeUforward_ 

For us, it would be easy to make 10–20 crores this year by just spending 2 months on AI. Launch something around AI, ride the hype, and with the brand we carry, that is easy money lying out there, along with a quick boost to ARR.

But for us, product means sustainability. We want to build something that is truly worth it, something that does not fade away the moment a competitor launches the next shiny thing by terming "XYZ is dead" 

Product + Content (taught by me) is our MOAT. Copying one is hard. Copying both together is even harder.

We will not chase hype. We will focus on building so well that even 5 years from now, we are still standing strong and doing well, instead of having chased quick money. Most influencers who turned founders in our space, have faded away, if you take a pause and think.  

Build in public, will keep sharing the thought process, everything we do, it's fun, this phase.

tweets.full_text:
Allbirds sold its brand to the company behind Ed Hardy for $39 million.
Then announced it’s becoming an AI company. Stock went up 600% in a day.

Here’s what’s actually going on- 

> They didn’t pivot the company. They sold the company: the name, the IP, everything, kept the empty Nasdaq shell, and stapled “AI” to it. You’re not buying Allbirds. You’re buying a ticker.

> They filed to legally strip the environmental obligations from their corporate charter. The certified B Corp that built its entire identity on sustainability is removing “environmental conservation public benefit” from its founding documents ( in a filing) to run energy hungry servers. 

> The $50M backing this is a convertible note from an investor nobody will name. Convertible notes dilute every existing shareholder when they convert. The conversion terms still haven’t been published. 

> The deal hasn’t closed. Shareholder vote is May 18. The stock moved 600% on a press release for a transaction that isn’t actually done.

> The CEO spent 40 years in outdoor apparel (North Face, Mountain Hardwear, Patagonia) He has never worked in tech and is now running a GPU leasing company.

This is a $4 billion company that was sold for $39 million to Ed Hardy’s parent and reborn as an AI stock overnight.

Long Island Iced Tea did this in 2017. Called itself Long Blockchain. Up 380% on the day. SEC charged insiders. Never built anything. Delisted.

Same playbook. Read the filing - not the headline.

tweets.full_text:
As of April 15, 2026

The AI Photonics sector lit up today, led by $SIVEF and $SIVE ripping 35% and 30%. Sivers Semiconductors announced a collaboration with Jabil to develop a 1.6T LRO transceiver module using Sivers' DFB InP lasers for hyperscale AI datacenters at 2.5x lower power draw. (https://t.co/j4drYtj4on)

$ALMU's +16% layered on top, digesting its $4M+ U.S. government contract win from Monday. The Jabil deal confirms DFB laser sourcing as a critical bottleneck in the 800G-to-1.6T migration. Transceiver module names lagging on tariff overhang, not demand breakdown.

$SIVEF: +35.23% 
$SIVE: +30.20% 
$ALMU: +16.62% 
$IQE: +12.97% 
$SOI: +6.19% 
$SLOIF: +4.57% 
$POET: +3.87% 
$CIEN: +1.83%
$NDX: +1.40% 
$FORM: +1.04% 
$SPX: +0.80% 
$TSEM: +0.71% 
$MRVL: +0.58% 
$TER: -0.15% 
$GFS: -0.21% 
$ATEYY: -0.47% 
$LWLG: -1.06% 
$AEHR: -1.56% 
$KEYS: -1.58% 
$COHR: -1.67% 
$BESIY: -1.81% 
$AAOI: -2.62% 
$GLW: -2.63% 
$LITE: -3.37% 
$VIAV: -3.73% 
$AXTI: -6.49%

1. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Photonic Laser Sources

SIVEF, SIVE, IQE, SOI, SLOIF: Today's clear winner bucket. Jabil deal is a direct read-through for InP photonics suppliers, with the market pricing in compound semiconductor wafer tightness as 1.6T module demand scales into 2H26.

AXTI, TSEM, GFS: AXTI down 6.5% on its own supply-demand timing mismatch, not thesis deterioration. TSEM and GFS caught a modest SiPho/specialty foundry bid but lack the direct InP laser catalyst.

2. Photonic Integrated Circuits & Early-Stage Materials

ALMU: Carrying Monday's $4M+ government contract momentum into today. Quantum dot laser and nonlinear photonic materials funding is direct AI datacom and quantum compute positioning. Watch the AngelTech panel April 20.

POET, LWLG, BESIY: Risk-on sympathy trade. Today's catalyst is InP-specific, so EO polymer and other platform stories get lower beta. No fundamental change.

3. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking

CIEN: Steadiest name in the group, lower tariff exposure than pure module assemblers. AAOI, LITE, COHR, GLW, VIAV all red on supply chain repricing under current trade policy. Demand thesis intact.

4. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure

MRVL: Holding well. PAM4 DSP silicon wins regardless of which laser or module vendor takes the socket. AEHR, FORM, KEYS, TER soft across the board; no direct read-through from a laser collaboration print. KEYS' new 220 GHz lightwave analyzer is strategically relevant but not a near-term catalyst today.

quoted_status.full_text:
As of April 14, 2026 (removed some players from the list)

The AI photonics and optical infrastructure sector is showing a sharp split today, with substrate and materials plays surging while early-stage and speculative names get hammered. $SOI led the watchlist with an +11.91% move, extending a parabolic run fueled by accelerating investor conviction in Soitec's Photonics-SOI substrate business for co-packaged optics and AI datacenter interconnects. Morningstar noted that Soitec's shares rose 50% in just five trading sessions as investors priced in a bullish outlook for the firm's silicon photonics segment. Morningstar (https://t.co/djE1rX2T9F)
The broader sector is being reshaped by a clear bifurcation: capital is rotating into names with tangible substrate and test infrastructure exposure to the AI optical buildout, while pre-revenue photonic integration plays are getting punished. $POET dropped after Wolfpack Research disclosed a short position, calling the company "an obvious stock promotion" and flagging potential PFIC tax liabilities for U.S. shareholders ahead of the April 15 filing deadline. https://t.co/RRfdqNGIy4 $SIVEF fell in sympathy as the short report reinforced broader skepticism toward pre-revenue photonic chip names. Meanwhile, SPX +2.21% and NDX +1.66% confirm this is a risk-on tape where the photonics laggards are underperforming on idiosyncratic, not macro, weakness.
$SOI: +11.91%
$ATEYY: +8.65%
$LWLG: +7.20%
$BESIY: +4.82%
$AEHR: +3.84%
$TSEM: +2.38%
$MRVL: +1.90%
$COHR: +1.80%
$AXTI: +1.10%
$FORM: 0.00%
$KEYS: -0.22%
$TER: -1.22%
$GFS: -1.28%
$GLW: -1.35%
$VIAV: -1.64%
$LITE: -2.15%
$IQE: -2.65%
$CIEN: -2.68%
$AAOI: -4.41%
$ALMU: -7.36%
$POET: -8.01%
$SIVEF: -11.50%
1. Compound Semiconductor Substrates and Foundries
$SOI, $BESIY, $TSEM, $AXTI: The substrate tier is where capital is concentrating. SOI's parabolic move reflects the market treating Photonics-SOI wafers as a structural bottleneck for CPO adoption. BESIY (BE Semiconductor) rode the same wave on die-attach and advanced packaging exposure. TSEM continues to benefit from specialty foundry demand, while AXTI held green on InP substrate scarcity narrative despite thin volume.
$IQE, $GFS: IQE gave back -2.65% as the OTC liquidity drag persists despite the same end-market tailwinds. GFS slipped -1.28%, still digesting the auto/industrial overhang even as its RF-SOI partnership with Soitec positions it well longer term.
2. Optical Transceivers and Active Networking
$COHR, $MRVL: Coherent and Marvell both green, tracking the 800G/1.6T transceiver cycle. COHR +1.80% is steady rather than explosive here, which suggests the easy money in pluggable transceiver names may already be priced.
$LITE, $CIEN, $AAOI: The transceiver and networking names that should be benefiting from the same AI optical build are instead lagging. LITE -2.15% and CIEN -2.68% are giving back recent gains. AAOI -4.41% continues to trade heavy, potentially reflecting profit-taking after its tariff-exemption-driven run.
$GLW, $VIAV: Passive optical infrastructure names drifting lower. Not broken, just not where the heat is today.
3. Semiconductor Equipment, Test and Infrastructure
$AEHR, $FORM, $KEYS, $TER, $ATEYY: AEHR +3.84% is the standout, continuing its recent strength on wafer-level burn-in demand tied to SiC and compound semi production ramps. ATEYY (Advantest) surged +8.65%, a strong signal that test infrastructure spending for advanced packaging and photonics ICs is accelerating. FORM flat, KEYS and TER slightly red but neither signaling anything concerning.
4. Early-Stage Photonic Materials and PICs
$LWLG, $ALMU, $POET, $SIVEF: LWLG bucked the early-stage selloff with a +7.20% move, potentially on renewed interest in EO polymer modulators as CPO architectures demand novel modulator solutions beyond conventional LiNbO3. ALMU -7.36% gave back gains on thin float dynamics. POET -8.01% is the direct casualty of the Wolfpack short report, which weaponized the PFIC tax angle right before the April 15 deadline. SIVEF -11.50% is the worst performer on the board, dragged down by contagion from the short seller thesis hitting the pre-revenue photonic integration cohort.

tweets.full_text:
A Big 4 firm just signed an alliance with a Bittensor subnet.

The official press release names "decentralized AI Bittensor blockchain" by name.

The firm is PwC France and Maghreb.

Number 2 in the Big 4. $56.9B in global revenue. 364,000 employees across 136 countries. The branch in this story has 7,000 people.

The subnet is Score. Subnet 44 on Bittensor. Operated by Manako Labs.

What Manako does:

• Connects to existing camera networks. No new hardware
• Operations leaders describe what they need in plain language
• System monitors, understands, and triggers real-world actions
• Lockdowns, dispatches, audit reports, workflow automations
• Not alerts to a dashboard. Actions executed in the world

There are 1 billion cameras watching the world right now. 21 billion connected sensors. Almost none of it drives action. That gap is billions of dollars in retail shrinkage, manufacturing downtime, and liability exposure every year.

PwC just signed up to fix it using a Bittensor subnet.

Quote from Jean-Thomas Ledore, Strategy Partner at PwC France and Maghreb:

"Physical AI is rapidly moving from emerging technology to an operational necessity."

That was story one.

Story two. Macrocosmos. Subnet 9. They published a research paper on April 13.

It's called ResBM. Residual Bottleneck Models.

It solves the single hardest problem in decentralized AI training. Bandwidth.

Training a frontier model means splitting it across many GPUs. Those GPUs send each other massive amounts of data every step. Datacenters do this with $10,000 fiber cables. Home internet is roughly 300 times slower. That gap is why every frontier model has been trained inside Google, Meta, OpenAI, and Anthropic.

ResBM compresses the data sent between GPUs by 128x.

With near zero loss in model quality.

The numbers from the paper:

• 2 billion parameter Llama-3 model
• Trained on 26 billion tokens of C4
• Uncompressed baseline: 21.75 perplexity
• ResBM at 100x compression: 21.60 perplexity (better than baseline)
• ResBM at 128x compression: 21.77 perplexity (basically tied)

In real training throughput on a consumer 80 Mbps line:

• Old way: 609 tokens per second
• ResBM: 7,837 tokens per second
• 12.8x speedup over the same internet connection

The architecture is already shipping into IOTA on Subnet 9.

Story three. Ridges AI. Subnet 62.

They build autonomous software engineering agents. Direct competitor to Cursor, Claude Code, Codex.

They hit 80% on SWE-Bench in roughly 45 days with under $1M paid to miners. Backed by Stillcore Capital. Powered by Chutes for compute.

Their consumer product is Ridgeline. $29 a month. Roughly 5 to 7 times cheaper than Claude Code or Codex at comparable benchmarks.

Today they shipped four production features:

• Rerun. Add context to a previous run, run it again
• Stop. Kill a current run mid-task. No wasted compute
• Collapsible chat view. All runs for one issue threaded
• Commit stats per run with diffs

The current top miner score on the network is 87.8%.

Three subnets. Three real shipments. Same week.

A Big 4 firm. A research breakthrough. A consumer product update.

None of this is vaporware. All of it is using existing Bittensor infrastructure today.

The story isn't TAO price. It's what's getting built on top of it.

quoted_status.full_text:
TSMC1Q決算、公式発表文（Grok翻訳）

台湾・新竹、2026年4月16日 ― TSMCは本日、2026年3月31日終了の第1四半期における連結業績を発表しました。売上高：NT$1,134.10億
純利益：NT$572.48億
希薄化後1株当たり利益：NT$22.08（ADR1単位当たり US$3.49）

前年同期比では、第1四半期売上高が35.1%増加、純利益および希薄化後EPSはともに58.3%増加しました。前四半期（2025年第4四半期）比では、売上高が8.4%増加、純利益が13.2%増加しました。すべての数値は、国際財務報告基準（TIFRS）に準拠した連結ベースで作成されています。米ドルベースでは、第1四半期売上高は 35.90億米ドルで、前年同期比40.6%増、前四半期比6.4%増となりました。利益率は以下の通りです：粗利益率：66.2%
営業利益率：58.1%
純利益率：50.5%

第1四半期の先端プロセス別ウェハー売上構成は以下の通りです：3nm：総ウェハー売上高の 25%
5nm：36%
7nm：13%

先進技術（7nmおよびそれより先端のプロセス）は、総ウェハー売上高の 74% を占めました。

https://t.co/yI7OJdhdi6

tweets.full_text:
先日のポストに「AIで翻訳を組み込めば翻訳コスト下がる（意訳）」みたいなリプ飛んできたけど、将来的にはそうなる可能性あるかもしれないけれど、現状じゃあそのAI翻訳された言語でそのままリリースして「翻訳がクソだ！」って言われたらどうすんだっつーの。

版権元（開発元）にその言語のネイティブが関わっていない以上、こっちは製品品質の担保が出来ねえんだから。
特にR18のノベルゲームみたいな物は国によって宗教や考え方の違い、エロがあるから所持してるだけで逮捕されるような国もあって、どこが地雷になるかわからんネタなのと、「品質保持」って概念がアイツら抜け落ちてんだよな。

user.name: KRAQ VOID
user.description: AI Filmmaker / Creative Director | Hedra Ambassador | Hailuo・Zopia・Invideo CPP 🌟 | Chroma Awards HM | Fairground | 最新AIによる映像表現の探求 | KRAQ VOID 📩 DM for Work

quoted_status.full_text:
Seedance 2.0 × Lovart リリース記念AI動画コンテストに参加します。 @lovart_jp さま @akiyoshisan さま、素晴らしい企画をありがとうございます！
──────────────── 
🎬 REBOOT : STILL HUNGRY
世界が何度壊れても、彼女だけが覚えている。

前作『REBOOT』で描いたのは、不条理な世界を彷徨い続ける彼女の姿。 今回は、そんな彼女の「ある一日」のスピンオフです。

行き先は選べない。でも、腹は減る。 
────────────────
海外のアウトドア料理動画に触発され、Seedance 2.0が得意とする「質感の暴力」をどこまで引き出せるか挑戦しました。

シズル感、スローモーション、そして時間停止——。 Seedance 2.0の圧倒的な表現力を、1分間に詰め込んでいます。

🎥 ワークフロー
LovartでSeedance 2.0を使用。 
15秒×4構成の各ブロックで複数パターンを生成し、計20本の中からベストを厳選。 
スピード感のあるカット編集と、AI特有の緩急を組み合わせる作業は、最高にクリエイティブな時間でした。

Lovartのアートボードは視認性が高く、構成の入れ替えが直感的で、全体を俯瞰しながら制作できるのが本当に使いやすかったです。

コンテスト応募は何度やってもドキドキしますが、こだわりを凝縮した1分間。 ぜひ、最後までご覧ください。

腹が減ってきたぜ!!!!!!!!!

🛠️ Tools
・AI Video : Lovart × Seedance 2.0 
・Image Gen : Nano Banana Pro 
・Music : Artlist 
・Editing : DaVinci Resolve
